worth_it_explanation: DoubleVerify's Fraud Lab is accredited by the Media Rating Council for both GIVT and SIVT detection. That accreditation only delivers value if the measurement is actually enabled, the GIVT and SIVT breakdown is being read correctly, and findings are connected to supply decisions. These checks run through the DV Pinnacle dashboard and relevant DSP settings. Each check surfaces a specific gap between what the Fraud Lab can detect and what the account is doing with that data.
intro: DoubleVerify's Fraud Lab measures invalid traffic (IVT) across two categories defined by the Media Rating Council (MRC). General invalid traffic (GIVT) covers traffic identified through routine filtration, such as data center signatures and known bot lists. Sophisticated invalid traffic (SIVT) covers harder-to-detect activity that mimics real user behavior, including ad stacking, pixel stuffing, and bot networks that cycle through real browser environments. The MRC accredits DoubleVerify for SIVT detection and filtration on desktop, mobile web, mobile in-app, and connected TV (CTV) environments. These checks confirm that fraud measurement is active across the advertiser's media, that GIVT and SIVT are being read as distinct signals, and that elevated findings are connected to supply-side exclusions or refund pursuit.

audit_checks.title: Fraud monitoring is not active across all media, leaving portions of spend without IVT measurement
audit_checks.impact_rationale: When fraud monitoring from DoubleVerify is not enabled on a line item or campaign, there is no IVT measurement for that spend. The advertiser cannot detect GIVT or SIVT on those placements, cannot pursue refunds for fraudulent impressions, and cannot exclude the supply sources generating them. Budget running without fraud coverage operates entirely on the DSP's baseline filtering, which varies by platform and does not provide the SIVT detection that MRC-accredited verification adds.

educate.base:
DoubleVerify's Fraud Lab measures invalid traffic (IVT) at the impression level by applying detection logic to each ad call. For that measurement to occur, DoubleVerify must be connected to the buying platform and activated on the relevant line items or campaigns. Without activation, the Fraud Lab has no visibility into what that portion of spend is delivering.

The Media Rating Council (MRC) defines two IVT categories. General invalid traffic (GIVT) covers non-human activity identified through routine means, such as known bot lists, data center IP ranges, and web crawlers. Sophisticated invalid traffic (SIVT) covers harder-to-detect activity that passes routine checks, including ad stacking, pixel stuffing, domain spoofing, and bot networks operating through real browser environments. The MRC accredits DoubleVerify for both GIVT and SIVT detection across desktop, mobile web, mobile in-app, and connected TV (CTV) environments, meaning DoubleVerify's methods have been audited against MRC's standards.

The value of that accreditation depends on coverage. A campaign with fraud monitoring active on display line items but not on video or CTV line items is partially covered. The uncovered line items generate spend with no IVT measurement and no record of fraud exposure.

A few signs point to coverage gaps in fraud monitoring:
  • The DV Pinnacle fraud summary shows spend that is not attributed to any measurement activation, or shows fewer campaigns than the advertiser is currently running
  • Video or CTV campaigns do not appear in the Fraud Lab reporting alongside display campaigns
  • The DSP shows active line items that have no corresponding DoubleVerify tag or integration active
You may want to cross-reference the campaign list in DV Pinnacle with the active campaign list in each DSP before concluding that coverage is complete.

site_type_overlays.ecommerce: For a retail advertiser, uncovered CTV or video campaigns represent spend driving brand awareness without fraud measurement. Those formats carry elevated IVT exposure because their higher cost-per-mille (CPM) makes each fraudulent impression more profitable to generate. You may want to confirm that DoubleVerify measurement is active on every format, not just display.
site_type_overlays.lead_gen: For a lead-generation advertiser, display and in-app mobile line items that lack DoubleVerify coverage are generating impression counts with no IVT filter beyond the DSP's baseline. You may want to verify that fraud monitoring is active on in-app mobile line items specifically, as that environment has no persistent cookie or device identifier and is harder for DSPs to filter on their own.

learn_more.source_title: MRC Standards and Guidelines: Invalid Traffic Detection (IVT) Guidelines
What is the IAB Tech Lab Spiders and Bots list?

educate.learn_more_note: DoubleVerify Pinnacle help documentation for activation setup and campaign tagging requires a partner login. Contact your DoubleVerify account team or log in to DV Pinnacle directly for setup guides.

investigate.intro: Confirm that DoubleVerify fraud monitoring is active across all campaigns and formats, not only the ones already visible in Fraud Lab reporting.

dv_pinnacle.label: DoubleVerify Pinnacle
steps: Log in to DV Pinnacle and navigate to the Fraud Lab section. Review the campaign list shown in the fraud summary dashboard.
Compare the campaign list in DV Pinnacle to the advertiser's full active campaign list in each connected DSP. Note any campaigns or line items that appear in the DSP but not in the Fraud Lab summary.
For each gap identified, check whether DoubleVerify tagging or integration is present on the line item in the DSP. A line item with no DV tag has no fraud measurement active.
Note the formats covered: display, video, CTV, in-app mobile. Confirm whether each format type is represented in Fraud Lab reporting or whether some formats show no fraud data despite running spend.

evaluate.logic: The practitioner compares the campaign and line item list in DV Pinnacle's Fraud Lab to the active campaign list in each connected DSP. Any active campaign or line item with spend that does not appear in Fraud Lab reporting and has no DoubleVerify tag or integration active is a gap. A gap is recorded with the campaign name, the DSP, the format type, and the estimated spend running without fraud coverage. Full coverage across all active campaigns and formats is a pass.
evaluate.fallback_message: Compare the DV Pinnacle campaign list to the active DSP campaign list above to see the evaluation.

generate.narrative_template: {{voice_found}} that {{site_name}}'s DoubleVerify fraud monitoring is not active across all active media. Portions of spend are running with no invalid traffic (IVT) measurement beyond the DSP's baseline filtering, leaving no record of fraud exposure and no basis for supply exclusions or refund pursuit on those placements. DoubleVerify is accredited by the Media Rating Council (MRC) for both general invalid traffic (GIVT) and sophisticated invalid traffic (SIVT) detection, but that accreditation only applies where measurement is active. {{voice_recommend}} confirming DoubleVerify tagging or integration is active on every campaign and format type, with particular attention to video, connected TV (CTV), and in-app mobile, which carry higher IVT exposure than display.

audit_checks.title: IVT reporting is not reviewed at the GIVT versus SIVT level, so the team cannot distinguish routine bot traffic from sophisticated fraud
audit_checks.impact_rationale: GIVT and SIVT require different responses. GIVT represents routine non-human traffic from known bots and data centers, which DSPs and exchanges already filter to varying degrees. SIVT represents activity that passes routine checks and requires behavioral detection to catch. A team reading only a combined IVT rate cannot tell whether the problem is filterable through standard means or whether it requires deeper intervention at the supply level. Without the breakdown, the response to an elevated IVT rate may target the wrong layer.

educate.base:
The Media Rating Council (MRC) defines GIVT and SIVT as distinct categories because they reflect different fraud mechanisms and require different responses. General invalid traffic (GIVT) consists of traffic identified through routine filtration: known bot lists, data center IP ranges, web crawlers, and similar standardized signals. SIVT consists of activity that passes routine filtration and requires advanced analysis to detect, including ad stacking, pixel stuffing, domain spoofing, and sophisticated bot networks that cycle through real browser environments and generate behavioral signals that resemble human users.

DoubleVerify's Fraud Lab, which is MRC-accredited for SIVT detection and filtration, reports GIVT and SIVT as separate metrics in DV Pinnacle. Reading them separately changes the diagnostic. An elevated GIVT rate on a particular supply source may indicate that the exchange or DSP's own filtering is not catching known bots, which points to a supply quality or configuration issue. An elevated SIVT rate on the same source indicates that traffic is passing standard bot lists and exhibits behavioral patterns that suggest fraud, which points to a different supply problem and warrants exclusion or a refund claim.

When a team reads only a combined IVT rate without breaking out GIVT from SIVT, the rate may look acceptable because one component is low while the other is high, or the team may apply the wrong intervention because they are not reading which type of fraud is driving the number.

A few signs point to GIVT and SIVT not being reviewed separately:
  • Fraud reviews reference only a single IVT percentage without distinguishing which portion is GIVT and which is SIVT
  • DV Pinnacle's Fraud Lab summary is the only view being used, without drilling into the GIVT and SIVT breakdowns by campaign or supply source
  • Refund claims have been pursued on GIVT findings but not on SIVT findings, or vice versa, without a documented rationale for the difference
You may want to review the Fraud Lab reporting at the campaign level and sort by SIVT rate specifically. SIVT is the harder category to detect and the more direct indicator of active fraud against the account.

site_type_overlays.ecommerce: For a retail advertiser, SIVT on display or video line items means that activity mimicking real users is consuming impressions and inflating delivery counts without contributing to product consideration or conversion. You may want to confirm whether the SIVT rate on high-spend placements correlates with an absence of conversion activity in those placements. That combination is a stronger signal than IVT rate alone.
site_type_overlays.lead_gen: For a lead-generation advertiser, GIVT on an in-app mobile line item may indicate a filtering gap at the exchange level, while SIVT on the same line item suggests activity designed to look like engagement. You may want to review whether in-app mobile placements with elevated SIVT rates show any form submission activity in the analytics tool. SIVT generating no corresponding form data is a direct indicator that the traffic is not real.

learn_more.source_title: MRC Standards and Guidelines: Invalid Traffic Detection (IVT) Guidelines
MRC Digital Measurement Accreditation: DoubleVerify SIVT Detection/Filtration

educate.learn_more_note: DoubleVerify Pinnacle reporting guides, including the GIVT and SIVT breakdown views in Fraud Lab, require a partner login. Log in to DV Pinnacle directly or contact your DoubleVerify account team for reporting documentation.

investigate.intro: Pull the Fraud Lab report in DV Pinnacle and confirm that GIVT and SIVT are being reviewed as separate metrics rather than as a combined IVT total.

dv_pinnacle.label: DoubleVerify Pinnacle
steps: Log in to DV Pinnacle and navigate to the Fraud Lab section. Open the fraud summary for the account.
Look for a breakdown of IVT into GIVT and SIVT components. In DV Pinnacle, the Fraud Lab reports these separately. Note whether both are present in the report view you are using.
If the current view shows only a combined IVT rate, switch to a more detailed report view or filter by metric type to surface the GIVT and SIVT breakdown by campaign and by supply source.
For each campaign or supply source with an elevated IVT rate, record the GIVT rate and the SIVT rate separately. Note which component is driving the overall IVT rate.
Record whether any campaign shows a SIVT rate above the account average. Flag those campaigns and their supply sources for the evaluation step.

evaluate.logic: The practitioner records whether Fraud Lab reporting is being reviewed at the GIVT and SIVT breakdown level, and whether any campaign shows a SIVT rate above the account average. Accounts where GIVT and SIVT are reviewed separately and where any SIVT findings are documented with a corresponding response (supply exclusion, refund claim, or documented decision not to act) pass. Accounts where only a combined IVT rate is tracked, or where the GIVT and SIVT breakdown exists in DV Pinnacle but is not being reviewed, are a fail recorded with the campaigns or supply sources showing elevated SIVT.
evaluate.fallback_message: Record the GIVT and SIVT rates by campaign or supply source above to see the evaluation.

generate.narrative_template: {{voice_found}} that {{site_name}}'s fraud reporting reviews a combined invalid traffic (IVT) rate without distinguishing general invalid traffic (GIVT) from sophisticated invalid traffic (SIVT). The Media Rating Council (MRC) defines these as separate categories requiring different responses: GIVT represents routine non-human traffic filterable through standard means, while SIVT represents activity that passes routine checks and indicates active fraud against the account. DoubleVerify's Fraud Lab reports both metrics separately in DV Pinnacle. {{voice_recommend}} reviewing the Fraud Lab GIVT and SIVT breakdown by campaign and by supply source, and establishing a practice of flagging supply sources where SIVT exceeds the account average for exclusion review or refund pursuit.

audit_checks.title: Elevated IVT findings are not connected to supply exclusions or refund pursuit, so fraudulent supply continues to receive budget
audit_checks.impact_rationale: Fraud measurement without action on findings allows the same supply sources to continue generating invalid impressions on future flights. The Fraud Lab identifies which supply sources have elevated IVT rates, but the identification alone does not stop the fraud. Without a process that connects Fraud Lab findings to DSP exclusion lists or refund claims filed with supply partners, the advertiser absorbs the cost of fraudulent impressions repeatedly.

educate.base:
DoubleVerify's Fraud Lab identifies supply sources with elevated invalid traffic (IVT) rates, including both general invalid traffic (GIVT) and sophisticated invalid traffic (SIVT). The identification produces a finding. Acting on that finding requires a separate step: either excluding the supply source from future buying in the DSP, or pursuing a refund for impressions already delivered against the advertiser's campaign.

Two distinct actions address IVT findings at the supply level. The first is a supply exclusion applied in the DSP. When a domain, app bundle, or exchange consistently shows elevated IVT in Fraud Lab reporting, adding it to the DSP's exclusion list removes that supply from future bidding. The exclusion prevents the same fraud from recurring without requiring the account to catch it again on the next flight.

The second is a refund or credit claim. Many DSPs and supply partners have make-good policies for IVT delivered above a specified threshold. DoubleVerify's Fraud Lab reporting provides the documentation needed to support those claims. The MRC's IVT standards inform what constitutes a valid finding for claim purposes, and verification vendors typically assist advertisers in filing claims against supply partners.

A few signs point to findings not being connected to action:
  • DV Pinnacle's Fraud Lab shows supply sources with IVT rates above the account average that are still active in the DSP's buying pool with no exclusion applied
  • No refund claims have been filed against supply partners despite Fraud Lab showing sustained IVT findings on specific domains or app bundles
  • The account's DSP exclusion list has not been updated since the Fraud Lab was connected, suggesting Fraud Lab findings have not been used to build or maintain the exclusion list
You may want to establish a regular cadence for reviewing Fraud Lab findings and connecting elevated supply sources to the DSP's exclusion list, even if refund claims are not pursued for every finding.

site_type_overlays.ecommerce: For a retail advertiser, IVT on placements driving traffic to product or checkout pages inflates session counts in downstream analytics without adding real purchase intent. You may want to confirm that supply sources with sustained IVT findings in Fraud Lab are excluded from line items targeting audiences in the lower funnel, where fraud has a direct distorting effect on conversion metrics.
site_type_overlays.lead_gen: For a lead-generation advertiser, IVT on in-app mobile or display line items inflates impression and click counts without producing form submissions. You may want to review whether supply sources flagged in Fraud Lab are also present in the placement report alongside the zero-conversion signal. A supply source with elevated IVT and zero conversions over multiple flights is a strong candidate for exclusion.

learn_more.source_title: MRC Standards and Guidelines: Invalid Traffic Detection (IVT) Guidelines
What is the IAB Tech Lab Spiders and Bots list?

educate.learn_more_note: DoubleVerify Pinnacle documentation for filing refund claims and exporting supply exclusion lists requires a partner login. Contact your DoubleVerify account team for guidance on claim procedures and DSP exclusion list exports.

investigate.intro: Identify supply sources with elevated IVT findings in DV Pinnacle and confirm whether those sources have been excluded in the DSP or whether refund claims have been filed.

dv_pinnacle.label: DoubleVerify Pinnacle
steps: Log in to DV Pinnacle and navigate to the Fraud Lab section. Open the supply-level or placement-level report that shows IVT rates by domain, app bundle, or exchange.

Sort the report by IVT rate or SIVT rate descending. Identify supply sources with an IVT rate above the account average. Note the supply source name or identifier, the IVT rate, and the impression volume.

For each flagged supply source, open the DSP that is buying from that supply source. Check the advertiser-level or campaign-level exclusion list for whether the domain, app bundle, or exchange is present. Record whether an exclusion exists.
Ask the account team whether any refund or make-good claims have been filed against supply partners for IVT findings. Note whether any such claims are in progress or have been completed.
Record supply sources that appear in Fraud Lab with sustained elevated IVT rates but have no corresponding exclusion in the DSP and no claim filed.

evaluate.logic: The practitioner records supply sources identified in Fraud Lab with IVT rates above the account average, then checks whether each has been excluded in the relevant DSP or whether a refund claim has been filed. A supply source that has been excluded, or where a claim has been filed with documented status, passes. A supply source with sustained elevated IVT that remains in the active buying pool with no exclusion and no claim is a fail recorded with the source name, the IVT rate, the impression volume, and the length of time the source has been flagged without action.
evaluate.fallback_message: Record the elevated IVT supply sources from DV Pinnacle and their exclusion or claim status above to see the evaluation.

generate.narrative_template: {{voice_found}} that {{site_name}}'s DoubleVerify Fraud Lab has identified supply sources with elevated invalid traffic (IVT) rates that have not been connected to supply exclusions in the DSP or to refund claims against supply partners. Fraud measurement that does not feed into supply decisions allows the same sources to continue receiving budget on future flights. {{voice_recommend}} reviewing the Fraud Lab supply report and adding sustained high-IVT domains and app bundles to the DSP's exclusion list, and {{voice_recommend}} working with the DoubleVerify account team to identify supply partners where a make-good claim is appropriate. Establishing a regular cadence for connecting Fraud Lab findings to DSP exclusion list updates reduces the likelihood of the same fraudulent supply recurring across flights.
